shadowDom.title: Shadow DOM
shadowDom.overview: Shadow DOM provides encapsulated DOM and styling for web components. Styles defined inside a component don't leak out, and external styles can't penetrate in.
implications: Global CSS selectors (like descendant selectors) can't pierce Shadow DOM boundaries
CSS custom properties (variables) CAN pierce Shadow DOM - use these for theming
Parts and slots enable controlled external styling exposure
shadowDom.themingApproach: CSS custom properties are the recommended theming approach as they pierce the Shadow DOM boundary.

ssr.title: Server-Side Rendering (SSR)
ssr.description: Server-side rendering with Next.js, Nuxt, Angular Universal

nextjs.name: Next.js
nextjs.approach: Use dynamic import with ssr: false for components that don't need SSR

nuxt.name: Nuxt.js
nuxt.approach: Use ClientOnly component or wrap in <ClientOnly>

ssr.commonPattern: Import web components dynamically on the client side to avoid SSR issues.
ssr.example: Use dynamic imports or framework-specific lazy loading mechanisms.

forms.title: Form Integration

approaches.name: Native Form Association
approaches.description: Associate custom elements with native forms using the 'form' attribute

approaches.name: Form Data Events
approaches.description: Listen to form events and manually sync data

approaches.name: Framework Bindings
approaches.description: Use React/Vue/Angular form libraries with custom inputs

validation: Use native HTML5 validation attributes where possible
Implement custom validation logic in framework wrapper components
Handle validation display in framework-specific ways

accessibility.title: Accessibility

principles.name: Keyboard Navigation
principles.description: Ensure all interactive elements are keyboard accessible

principles.name: ARIA Labels
principles.description: Use ARIA attributes when native semantics aren't sufficient

principles.name: Focus Management
principles.description: Manage focus appropriately for modals, dialogs, and dynamic content

testing: Test with screen readers (NVDA, VoiceOver, JAWS)
